was held in September 2018 at the Eissportzentrum Oberstdorf. It is part of the [2018–19 ISU Challenger Series](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_ISU_Challenger_Series "2018–19 ISU Challenger Series").[[1]](#cite_note-annoucement-1) Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance").

Figure skating competition held in Oberstdorf

The **2018 CS [Nebelhorn Trophy](/wiki/Nebelhorn_Trophy "Nebelhorn Trophy")** was held in September 2018 at the Eissportzentrum Oberstdorf. It is part of the [2018–19 ISU Challenger Series](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_ISU_Challenger_Series "2018–19 ISU Challenger Series").[[1]](#cite_note-annoucement-1) Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance").

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_CS_Nebelhorn_Trophy&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Records")]

For the all-time highest scores, see [List of highest scores in figure skating](/wiki/List_of_highest_scores_in_figure_skating "List of highest scores in figure skating").

The following new [ISU best scores](/wiki/List_of_highest_scores_in_figure_skating#Progression_of_men's_highest_scores "List of highest scores in figure skating") were set during this competition:

| Event | Component | Skater(s) | Score | Date | Ref |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Ladies](#Ladies) | Short program | [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") [Alina Zagitova](/wiki/Alina_Zagitova "Alina Zagitova") | **79.93** | 27 September 2018 | [[18]](#cite_note-isu-progress-L-SP-18) |
| Free skating | **158.50** | 28 September 2018 | [[19]](#cite_note-isu-progress-L-FS-19) |
| Total score | **238.43** | [[20]](#cite_note-isu-progress-L-TO-20) |
